position: at phase boundary — Phase 1 done, Phase 2 not yet started

completed_this_session.item: Phase 1 execute — Plan 01-03 (git history rewrite + force-push)
completed_this_session.note: Original SHAs rewritten by filter-repo; force-pushed twice — initial scrub, then again after gap-close

human_actions_pending.action: Update display/secrets.py on host AND on Pico with the NEW rotated WiFi password
human_actions_pending.context: During Phase 1 the router password was rotated but the on-host secrets.py still contains the pre-rotation strings. Any on-device test in Phase 2+ requires a working WiFi connection, which needs the current password in secrets.py.

session_lessons.lesson: When writing a SUMMARY that documents a credential/secret redaction, do NOT quote the redacted strings literally — refer to them by description or placeholder
session_lessons.evidence: The initial 01-03-SUMMARY.md quoted both credential strings in a fenced code block showing the git-filter-repo replacements file content. This survived past the filter-repo pass (which had already run) and re-leaked what was just scrubbed. Verifier caught it; fix required a second amend + force-push cycle. Future rule: describe redactions structurally, do not enumerate the strings.

notable_deviations.impact: Rotation (out-of-band) was the actual security mitigation; filter-repo is documentation-cleanup for future clones.

next_action: Start Phase 2 (Carousel + Weather) via `/gsd:discuss-phase 2`. Phase 2 has `UI hint: yes` in ROADMAP.md so `/gsd:ui-phase 2` is also a reasonable first step to lock the page-dot layout and Weather view geometry before discussing.
context_notes: Whole session was one continuous run from `/gsd-resume-work` through Phase 1 complete. Project uses `mode: yolo`, `granularity: coarse`, MVP mode, no research per phase, plan_checker + verifier enabled. Executed inline (not via gsd-executor worktree agents) because all Phase 1 plans required on-device human verification — worktree isolation adds friction without value for hardware-in-the-loop work. This pattern likely applies to Phase 2's on-device checkpoints too, but Phase 2 might benefit from more automation on the pure code-restructuring parts (view module extraction).
